title: Protective service associate professionals n.e.c.
description: Examine, evaluate, and investigate eligibility for or conformity with laws and regulations governing contract compliance of licenses and permits, and perform other compliance and enforcement inspection and analysis activities not classified elsewhere. Inspect and investigate sources of pollution to protect the public and environment and ensure conformance with Federal, State, and local regulations and ordinances. Monitor and evaluate compliance with equal opportunity laws, guidelines, and policies to ensure that employment practices and contracting arrangements give equal opportunity without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, age, or disability. (Based on 42 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Patrol and guard courthouses, assigned areas, or transportation hubs to provide security, enforce laws, maintain order, monitor suspicious individuals, and respond to emergencies.
Provide directions to passengers and respond to inquiries regarding their travel, security procedures, and ensure compliance with regulations, including reviewing tickets and identification.
Perform security screenings of passengers and baggage, including pat-down searches, x-ray inspections, and monitoring alarms to ensure compliance with safety regulations and detect prohibited items.
Monitor traffic in areas such as courthouses or checkpoints, ensuring compliance with laws, addressing violations, and providing assistance as needed.
Investigate suspicious or illegal activities, including examining the facts of incidents, determining eligibility for compliance with laws, and conducting interviews.
Respond to medical emergencies, including administering first aid and coordinating with emergency services for additional aid when necessary.
Prepare reports and maintain records documenting incidents, activities, and compliance with regulations and procedures.
Supervise and coordinate law enforcement personnel, including training staff, preparing schedules, and maintaining compliance with departmental standards.
Communicate with other law enforcement agencies and court personnel regarding investigations, legal procedures, and the enforcement of laws and regulations.
Inform and educate the community on public safety issues, respond to citizen questions, and maintain community relations to build trust and cooperation.
Notify appropriate personnel about security breaches and investigate claims related to illegal activities, ensuring that laws and regulations are adhered to.
Evaluate and interpret laws and regulations, inform personnel and the public of changes, and enforce compliance to protect public interests and safety.
Detain individuals found in violation of the law or suspected of criminal activity and ensure proper documentation and legal proceedings are followed.
Apprise supervisors of ongoing security situations, convey important information during emergencies, and operate communication equipment for coordination.
Conduct inspections of facilities, equipment, and personnel, ensuring safety standards are met, and investigating reports of misconduct to protect the integrity of operations.
Monitor compliance with customs laws, detect contraband, and conduct necessary investigations to maintain legal operations and public safety.
Assist in the investigation of tax compliance issues, ensuring adherence to regulations and resolving discrepancies with clients or taxpayers.
Examine financial practices to ensure compliance with regulations and investigate suspicious banking or financial activities to maintain institutional integrity.
Prepare legal documents and notifications regarding violation of regulations, ensuring that appropriate actions are taken against tax evasion and compliance failures.
Develop and implement department policies and procedures related to security, compliance, and law enforcement operations.
Conduct community outreach programs and initiatives aimed at educating citizens about public safety, crime prevention, and civic responsibility.
Maintain knowledge of regulatory changes and assist in developing training programs for staff regarding compliance with laws and safety procedures.

Investigate employment practices or alleged violations of laws to document and correct discriminatory factors, and conduct surveys and evaluate findings to determine if systematic discrimination exists.

Review and evaluate applications for registration of products containing dangerous materials, or for pollution control discharge permits, and determine requirements applying to treatment, storage, shipment, or disposal of potentially hazardous production-related waste.

Provide information, technical assistance, or training to supervisors, managers, or employees on topics such as employee supervision, hiring, grievance procedures, or staff development, and provide pre-, ongoing, and post-inspection follow-up assistance to governmental inspectors.
Prepare reports of activities, evaluations, recommendations, or decisions, and prepare reports related to investigations of equal opportunity complaints.

Inspect government property, such as construction sites or public housing, to ensure compliance with contract specifications or legal requirements, and monitor follow-up actions in cases where violations were found, reviewing compliance monitoring reports.

Conduct research on hazardous waste management projects to determine the magnitude of problems and treatment or disposal alternatives and costs, and participate in the development of spill prevention programs and hazardous waste rules and regulations, recommending corrective actions for hazardous waste problems.

Analyze and implement state, federal or local requirements as necessary to maintain approved pretreatment, pollution prevention, and storm water runoff programs, and monitor the implementation and impact of guidelines for nondiscriminatory employment practices.
Interview persons involved in equal opportunity complaints to verify case information, and meet with persons involved in equal opportunity complaints to arbitrate and settle disputes.

Conduct research to inform individuals and groups of pollution control regulations and inspection findings, explaining how problems can be corrected, and inform health professionals, property owners, and the public about harmful properties and related problems of water pollution and contaminated wastewater.

Investigation of complaints and suspected violations regarding illegal dumping, pollution, pesticides, product quality, or labeling laws, and verify that hazardous chemicals are handled, stored, and disposed of in accordance with regulations.

Coordinate, monitor, or revise complaint procedures to ensure timely processing and review of complaints, and investigate complaints and suspected violations regarding illegal dumping, pollution, pesticides, product quality, or labeling laws.

Examine permits, licenses, applications, and records to ensure compliance with licensing requirements, and evaluate applications, records, or documents to gather information about eligibility or liability issues.
Compile and maintain regulatory documentation databases or systems, and monitor status of loans and accounts to ensure that payments are up to date.

Prepare and process import and export documentation according to customs regulations, laws, or procedures, and stay abreast of changes in import or export laws or regulations by reading current literature, attending meetings or conferences, or conferring with colleagues.
Prepare, organize, and maintain inspection records, ensuring proper safety precautions, rules, regulations, and practices are observed.
Respond to questions and inquiries, such as those concerning service charges and capacity fees.

Review company contracts to determine actions required to meet governmental equal opportunity provisions, and determine the legal implications of the production, supply, or use of ozone-depleting substances or equipment containing such substances.

Research and keep informed of pertinent information and developments in areas such as EPA laws and regulations, and engage with community representatives to develop technical assistance agreements in accordance with governmental regulations.
Collect, identify, evaluate, or preserve case evidence, and investigate applications for special licenses or permits.

Investigate allegations concerning license or permit violations, and determine sites and violation reports to investigate, coordinating compliance and enforcement activities with other government agencies.
Prepare and process payroll information, maintaining financial calculations and preparing bank deposits as necessary.
Conduct laboratory tests on samples collected, such as analyzing the content of contaminated wastewater, to inform regulatory compliance.

Monitor investigations of suspected offenders to ensure that they are conducted in accordance with constitutional requirements; advise customers on import and export restrictions, tariff systems, insurance requirements, quotas, or other customs-related matters.

Complete death certificates, including the assignment of cause and manner of death. Observe and record the positions and conditions of bodies and related evidence. Collect and document any pertinent medical history information. Observe, record, and preserve any objects or personal property related to deaths, including objects such as medication containers and suicide notes. Complete reports and forms required to finalize cases. Interview persons present at death scenes to obtain information useful in determining the manner of death. Locate and document information regarding the next of kin, including their relationship to the deceased and the status of notification attempts. Provide information concerning the circumstances of death to relatives of the deceased. Confer with officials of public health and law enforcement agencies to coordinate interdepartmental activities. Inventory personal effects recovered from bodies, such as jewelry or wallets. Coordinate the release of personal effects to authorized persons. Arrange for the next of kin to be notified of deaths. Prepare legal documentation such as affidavits related to death investigations and trials. Conduct medicolegal examinations and autopsies to identify victims and assess signs of trauma.

Testify at inquests, hearings, and court trials, ensuring evidence and exhibits are handled correctly during court proceedings. Compile and present evidence for court actions and ensure compliance with standard operating procedures for legal investigations and trials. Investigate facts and law of cases and search pertinent sources to prepare cases. Maintain documentation or reports on security-related incidents associated with investigations. Provide assistance to the public, including directions to court offices, while maintaining courtroom security and cleanliness.

Monitor compliance with standard operating procedures related to physical security and risk management in enforcement activities. Recommend methods to reduce potential fraud losses and investigate any incidents of financial or regulatory violations.

Euthanize rabid, unclaimed, or severely injured animals. Examine animals for injuries or malnutrition and arrange for necessary medical treatment as part of wildlife conservation efforts. Conduct investigations regarding crop, property, or habitat damage, including pollution cases affecting wildlife.

Collaborate with local law enforcement agencies and coordinate departments to assist in wildlife enforcement programs. Participate in search-and-rescue operations when necessary and provide hunter or trapper safety training to the public.
Investigate hunting accidents or reports of fish or game law violations. Address civic groups or media regarding wildlife conservation regulations and promote environmental safety through education.
Administer investigative techniques including private investigations related to wildlife and enforce regulations governing hunting and fishing activities.
Issue licenses, permits, or other documentation pertinent to compliance with environmental and wildlife laws. Recommend new or improved processes to reduce risk exposure within the field.
Conduct personal background investigations as part of evaluating compliance with laws and regulations governing various professions and the environmental impacts of commercial operations.
Participate in firefighting efforts related to protect public safety in emergencies. Respond to critical incidents and efficiently manage incidents as they arise.
Prepare and supervise activities of seasonal workers or volunteers involved in wildlife management efforts or safety training programs.
Capture and remove stray, uncontrolled, or abused animals from undesirable conditions, using nets, nooses, or tranquilizer darts as necessary.
Investigate reports of animal attacks or animal cruelty, interviewing witnesses, collecting evidence, and writing reports.

Prepare and maintain required records, including logs of parking enforcement activities and records of contested citations; investigate and answer complaints regarding contested parking citations, determining their validity and routing them appropriately; write reports documenting observations made during investigations.

Inspect establishments housing animals for compliance with laws, examine animal licenses, issue warnings or citations for animal-related offenses, and contact police to report violations and request arrests.
Educate the public about animal welfare, and animal control laws and regulations, and prepare for prosecutions related to animal treatment, giving evidence in court as needed.
Maintain assigned equipment and supplies, such as citation computers, and animal control resources, and keep records of impoundments and dispositions of animals.
Examine documentation, such as mortgages and liens, to verify legal compliance and prepare reports of findings, documenting any title encumbrances encountered during investigations.
Observe and document activities of individuals to detect unlawful acts or to obtain evidence using binoculars and cameras, and retrieve and examine records where necessary.
Monitor and enforce courtroom rules of behavior, maintain security of sensitive materials, and oversee juror privacy and courtroom order during trials.
Inspect recreational facilities and equipment for safety hazards and cleanliness, and provide relevant information to the public regarding these inspections.
Patrol premises to prevent or detect intrusion and preserve order, documenting observations made while on patrol and reporting hazards or disturbances.
Direct work of contract security officers and ensure adherence to safety standards and procedures, conducting audits to identify procedural deficiencies.
Confer with law enforcement and other officials to exchange information, resolve problems, and collaborate in investigations as necessary.
Review and adjust security equipment to ensure it is operational, and document security transactions, using technology as necessary to facilitate these tasks.
Staff recruitment, training, and scheduling for security personnel, ensuring team effectiveness and compliance with protocols.
Ensure compliance with laws and regulations pertaining to animal control operations, including the organization of the adoption of unclaimed animals.
Monitor emergency and non-emergency calls, determine response requirements, and dispatch appropriate services while maintaining communication with responders and the public.
Relay information to law enforcement and emergency services, and provide emergency medical instructions to callers when necessary.

Interview credit applicants by telephone or in person to obtain personal and financial data needed to complete credit report; compile and analyze credit information gathered by investigation, and keep records of customers' charges and payments.

Verify ownership and transaction information and dividend distribution instructions to ensure conformance with governmental regulations, using stock records and reports. Prepare reports summarizing daily transactions and earnings for individual customer accounts, and compute total holdings, dividends, interest, and allocate appropriate payments to customers.

Interview loan applicants to obtain personal and financial data and to assist in completing applications, verify and examine information and accuracy of loan application and closing documents, and prepare, type, and submit loan applications with recommendation for underwriting approval.
Contact customers to inform them of acceptance or rejection of applications, and answer questions and advise customers regarding loans and transactions.

Assure compliance with regulations by reviewing insurance policies and applications, inspecting and monitoring operations to confirm adherence to state regulations and company policies, and verifying customer data for accuracy and completeness.

Monitor establishment activities to ensure adherence to all regulations and company policies, investigate and correct errors upon customer request, and provide customer service, including limited instructions on proceeding with claims and referrals.

Provide oversight or security by monitoring casino operations for irregular activities, developing and maintaining logs of surveillance observations, and reporting all violations and suspicious behaviors to supervisors.
Organize or work with detailed office or warehouse records, using computers to enter, access, search or retrieve data, and maintain files for each policyholder.
Process applications, monitor account compliance, collect payments, and prepare insurance claim forms or related documents for submission and review.
Prepare and maintain documentation for loan and credit processes, including acting as oversight or security agent for management or customers.
Schedule and coordinate transfer and delivery of documents or securities between departments, customers, or companies to ensure accuracy and compliance with regulations.

emerging_tasks.task: Read and review subordinates' reports to ensure legal standards are met and there are no mistakes.
Complete or review death certificates, including the assignment of cause and manner of death.
Develop guidelines for nondiscriminatory employment practices, such as affirmative action plans and equal opportunity employment policies.
Observe and record the positions and conditions of bodies and related evidence at the scene, through documentation or photographs.
Train employees on equal opportunity laws, guidelines, or policies, such as discrimination, diversity, harassment, or affirmative action.
Use emerging technologies, such as drones, for remote or automated inspections.
Deliver educational presentations on wildlife topics, such as animal safety, at schools and community events.
Manage attorneys' calendars and schedule meetings.
Operate drones for surveillance of large areas and tracking of wildlife.
Perform traffic control duties such as setting up barricades and temporary signs, placing bags on parking meters to limit their use, or directing traffic or pedestrians.
Pick up deceased animals from roadways and dispose of the animal carcasses.
Request, review, and summarize relevant records for the cases.
Serve documents to parties named in legal proceedings.
Testify at court hearings when citations are contested.
Transport injured or ill wildlife to rescues.
Use advanced technology, such as drones, GPS trackers, and surveillance cameras, to facilitate investigations.
Use drone technology for surveillance and loss prevention.
